title: Oxidative Stress Reversibly Impairs Deubiquitylase Activity in Aging Brain — Antioxidant NACET Restores Proteostasis
description: A Nature Communications study (published April 21, confirmed on Fight Aging! May 1) used activity-based proteomics in mouse and killifish brains to show that DUB enzyme activity declines ~40% with age due to thiol oxidation, preceding proteasome failure. Critically, the antioxidant N-acetylcysteine ethyl ester (NACET) fully restored DUB activity and proteasome function in aged animals, identifying a potentially reversible early driver of proteostasis collapse in the aging brain and flagging USP7 as a specific therapeutic target.

title: Infinite Epigenetics Acquires Tally Health: Largest Epigenetic Age Testing Consolidation Creates Integrated Longevity Platform
description: Announced April 29, Infinite Epigenetics (parent of TruDiagnostic) acquired David Sinclair co-founded Tally Health in the largest consolidation in epigenetic age testing to date, uniting clinical-grade methylation diagnostics with a consumer-facing supplement and testing platform. The deal creates the world's largest private adult DNA methylation dataset, positioning the company to move epigenetics from research and lifestyle applications into mainstream preventive healthcare with an integrated test-analyze-intervene loop.

title: RAPA-EX-01 Trial: Weekly Rapamycin May Attenuate Exercise Gains in Older Adults — Challenges Cycling Hypothesis
description: Results from the RAPA-EX-01 randomized trial (40 sedentary adults aged 65-85, Auckland) showed that once-weekly rapamycin 6 mg did not enhance — and in sensitivity analyses modestly attenuated — functional improvements from a 13-week home exercise program, with placebo outperforming rapamycin on chair-stand, 6-minute walk, grip strength, and quality-of-life endpoints. The finding challenges the popular 'cycling hypothesis' and suggests rapamycin's 62-hour half-life prevents a clean separation between drug and exercise anabolic windows, with the longevity community debating whether longer trials at different dosing schedules would yield different results.

title: High-Altitude Chronic Hypoxia Drives Accelerated Immune Aging — Multi-Organ Atlas Confirms Systemic Aging Trajectory
description: A Science Advances study published April 22 and highlighted by Fight Aging! on April 30 characterized immune landscapes in Tibetan populations at 3,656 m and 5,070 m elevation, finding marked enrichment of aging-associated immune cells (exhausted T cells, age-associated B cells) and elevated neutrophils compared to low-altitude cohorts — a pattern conserved in mouse hypoxia models and further showing accelerated gut aging trajectories via Stereo-seq. The findings reinforce environmental epigenetic aging theories and point to cytokine-driven systemic coordination as a shared aging mechanism, complementing the Rockefeller 7-million-cell atlas published in February.

title: Annovis Phase 2/3 Buntanetap Alzheimer's Data Published in Nature NPJ Dementia — Phase 3 Now 80% Enrolled
description: Annovis Bio published its 351-patient Phase 2/3 results for oral buntanetap in Nature NPJ Dementia on April 28, showing statistically significant, dose-dependent cognitive improvement (ADAS-Cog11) in pTau217-positive mild Alzheimer's patients alongside reductions in neurotoxic proteins and neuroinflammation biomarkers. The pivotal Phase 3 trial is now approximately 80% enrolled across 84 U.S. sites, keeping the company on track for a 6-month symptomatic data readout in the second half of 2026 — a significant near-term clinical milestone for an oral anti-neurodegeneration therapy.

title: Oxidative Stress Causes Reversible Decrease of Deubiquitylase Activity in Aging Vertebrate Brains
description: Activity-based proteomics in mouse and killifish brains identified a ~40% age-dependent decline in DUB enzyme activity due to thiol oxidation, preceding proteasome failure. In vivo antioxidant treatment with NACET restored DUB activity and improved proteasome function in aged animals.

title: Fight Aging!: FMT From Young Mice Suppresses MDM2 Expression and Prevents Liver Cancer in Aging Controls
description: A mouse study found that fecal microbiota transplantation (FMT) from young donors prevented liver cancer development in aging recipients (0/8 vs 2/8 controls), suppressed MDM2 protein levels, and reversed molecular aging hallmarks including inflammation, fibrosis, and telomere attrition in liver tissue.

title: NPR: Eric Topol on 'Super Aging' — Science vs. Grift in the Longevity Industry
description: Cardiologist Eric Topol published his book Super Agers and appeared on NPR May 1 arguing that healthspan — not lifespan — is the correct optimization target, and that lifestyle factors (exercise, sleep, social connection, reduced inflammation) and AI-enabled prevention outperform most commercial longevity products. He expressed concern about unregulated peptides and supplement marketing outpacing evidence.

title: High Altitude Populations Exhibit Features of Accelerated Immune Aging
description: Human immune profiling at 3,656 m and 5,070 m Tibetan elevation found significant enrichment of exhausted T cells, age-associated B cells, and elevated neutrophils versus low-altitude populations. Mouse models under simulated 5,000 m hypoxia confirmed coordinated niche interactions between aging-associated immune cells and intestinal epithelial cells, suggesting accelerated systemic aging trajectories from chronic hypoxic stress.

title: Infinite Epigenetics Acquires Tally Health to Create World's Largest Epigenetic Data Platform
description: TruDiagnostic parent Infinite Epigenetics acquired Sinclair co-founded Tally Health in the largest epigenetic age testing consolidation to date, combining clinical-grade DNA methylation diagnostics with a consumer platform and the world's largest private adult methylation dataset. Tally will remain a standalone consumer brand under CEO Melanie Goldey.

title: Longevity Technology Week 18 Roundup: Epigenetic Consolidation, Genflow SIRT6 Deal, SuperAge Games, Endlyz Seed 2
description: Week 18 coverage highlighted: Infinite Epigenetics/Tally Health acquisition; Genflow Biosciences partnering with Acuitas Therapeutics on SIRT6 mRNA LNP delivery (fully-funded, non-dilutive); Endlyz Therapeutics closing Seed 2 for lysosome-targeting Parkinson's therapies; SuperAge Games debut in NYC Nov 7; and Smith+Nephew AGILI-C knee implant showing 5-year superiority over standard care.

title: Annovis Bio Publishes Buntanetap Phase 2/3 Alzheimer's Results in Nature NPJ Dementia
description: Annovis published 351-patient Phase 2/3 data for oral buntanetap showing dose-dependent cognitive improvement (ADAS-Cog11) in pTau217-positive early Alzheimer's patients and reductions in neurotoxic proteins and neuroinflammation biomarkers. The pivotal Phase 3 trial is now ~80% enrolled with 6-month symptomatic data expected in H2 2026.

title: SuperAge Launches World's First Longevity Fitness Competition — SuperAge Games, NYC November 7
description: Super Age announced the inaugural SuperAge Games in New York City on November 7, 2026, an eight-trial circuit competition designed by longevity researchers to test cardiovascular fitness, endurance, strength, neurological function, balance, and social capacity through biomarker assessment. The event reframes athletic competition around healthspan durability rather than peak performance, targeting competitors aged 50-70.

title: Endlyz Therapeutics Closes Seed 2 Round to Advance Lysosome-Targeting Parkinson's Therapies
description: London-based Endlyz Therapeutics, backed by AbbVie, Dementia Discovery Fund, Oxford Science Enterprises, and Parkinson's UK, closed its Seed 2 financing to develop two lysosome-targeting drug candidates aimed at clearing cellular debris contributing to Parkinson's pathology. An additional $2.2 million from the Michael J. Fox Foundation supports biomarker development for early target engagement measurement.

title: RAPA-EX-01 Trial: Rapamycin May Attenuate Functional Exercise Gains in Older Adults
source: Journal of Cachexia, Sarcopenia and Muscle / NeuroAge Therapeutics
description: The RAPA-EX-01 randomized trial (n=40, age 65-85) found once-weekly rapamycin 6 mg did not enhance and in sensitivity analyses modestly attenuated functional gains from a 13-week home exercise program versus placebo, with placebo trending better on chair-stand, 6-minute walk, grip strength, and quality-of-life measures. Four epigenetic aging clocks showed no significant differences, though PCGrimAge trended slightly younger in the rapamycin arm.

title: Genflow Biosciences Partners with Acuitas Therapeutics for SIRT6 mRNA LNP Delivery — Fully-Funded Collaboration
description: Genflow Biosciences entered a fully-funded, non-dilutive collaboration with Acuitas Therapeutics (whose LNP technology underpinned COVID-19 mRNA vaccines) to package Genflow's centenarian-variant SIRT6 mRNA into lipid nanoparticles for preclinical evaluation. The deal provides Genflow access to a clinically validated delivery platform for a gene therapy targeting DNA repair and metabolic aging without diluting shareholders.

title: FDA Grants Faster Pathway for 4P004 Knee Osteoarthritis Drug — Inflammation MOTION Phase 2a Trial Underway
description: The FDA granted an accelerated development pathway to 4P004, a multi-mechanism anti-inflammatory drug candidate targeting inflammation, pain, tissue breakdown, and joint repair simultaneously in knee osteoarthritis. The INFLAM MOTION Phase 2a trial is running across Europe and North America with topline results expected in early 2027.

title: Aspen Neuroscience Sasineprocel Phase 1/2a ASPIRO Trial: 12-Month Data Show Clinically Meaningful Parkinson's Benefit
description: Aspen Neuroscience presented 12-month Phase 1/2a ASPIRO trial data for sasineprocel (ANPD001) at the AD/PD 2026 conference showing an average of ~2 additional hours of Good ON time per day, MDS-UPDRS Part III OFF improvements of 13.5-15.5 points, and PDQ-39 quality-of-life gains of 28.5-51.6% across dose cohorts with no serious adverse events. FDOPA PET imaging confirmed cell engraftment and survival; a Phase 3 trial is planned for late 2026.

title: BioAge Labs BGE-102 Phase 1 Results: Best-in-Class NLRP3 Inhibitor Achieves ~86% hsCRP Reduction in Aging-Inflammation Target
description: BioAge reported full Phase 1 data for BGE-102, an orally available brain-penetrant NLRP3 inhibitor, showing 85-86% median hsCRP reductions at both 60 mg and 120 mg once-daily doses in participants with obesity and elevated inflammation, with 87-93% achieving normalized hsCRP below the 2 mg/L cardiovascular risk threshold. Phase 2 cardiovascular risk and diabetic macular edema trials are planned for mid-2026, with BioAge positioning BGE-102 as a therapeutic targeting inflammaging — a core hallmark of biological aging.

title: Bemdaneprocel (BlueRock) Phase 1 Three-Year Data at AAN 2026: Cell Therapy Benefits Sustained in Parkinson's
description: BlueRock Therapeutics presented three-year follow-up from the Phase 1 exPDite study showing bemdaneprocel continued to control Parkinson's motor symptoms at the higher dose, with transplanted dopaminergic neuron precursors confirmed engrafted on PET imaging and quality of life remaining stable. The ongoing Phase 3 exPDite-2 trial is enrolling ~102 participants across the U.S., Canada, and Australia.

title: Mesoblast Acquires CAR Platform from Mayo Clinic Spinout to Engineer Precision-Enhanced Cell Therapies
description: Mesoblast acquired chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) platform technology from a Mayo Clinic spinout to enhance targeting precision of its mesenchymal stem cell (MSC) therapies for inflammatory and autoimmune diseases. The deal positions Mesoblast's rexlemestrocel platform to move from systemic inflammation suppression toward tissue-specific biological correction, extending potential applications into aging-related chronic inflammation.

title: Osteoboost Raises $8M to Scale FDA-Cleared Prescription Wearable for Bone Density in Women
description: Osteoboost Health closed an $8 million round led by Ambit Health Ventures to scale manufacturing and expand clinical research for its FDA-cleared vibration therapy wearable, which slowed bone density loss by 85% in the spine and 55% in the hips in a 12-month clinical trial. With over 2,500 prescribing physicians and prescriptions already active at 30+ academic medical centers, the device targets the osteopenia-to-osteoporosis progression gap.

title: Global Aging Research and Drug Discovery Meeting 2025 Report: Field Reaches 'Actionable Interventions' Turning Point
description: The 12th ARDD meeting report published in Aging-US highlighted key themes: epigenome as central aging regulator; partial cellular reprogramming as a path to organ-wide rejuvenation; new generations of multi-modal biological aging clocks; AI-driven drug discovery; and a consensus 'disease-first' regulatory strategy for validating aging interventions. The report concluded aging science has shifted from description to actionable therapeutic intervention.

title: Rapamycin Attenuates Physical Performance Improvements With Exercise in Older Adults — Journal of Cachexia Study
source: NAD.com / Journal of Cachexia, Sarcopenia and Muscle
description: A study published in the Journal of Cachexia, Sarcopenia and Muscle found rapamycin reduced 30-second chair-stand performance, grip strength by ~2.6 lbs, and worsened self-reported quality-of-life scores compared to placebo in older adults combining the drug with exercise, raising questions about the popular off-label longevity practice of combining mTOR inhibition with resistance training.

title: Senolytics 2026: Over 30 Active Trials — AFFIRM-LITE Fisetin Phase 2 Results Expected This Year
description: A comprehensive review documented more than 30 active clinical trials for senolytic and senomorphic interventions as of early 2026, spanning indications from diabetic macular edema and pulmonary fibrosis to Alzheimer's and general frailty. The Mayo Clinic's AFFIRM-LITE Phase 2 fisetin trial in adults over 70 with frailty is expected to report results in 2026, providing the first rigorous placebo-controlled senolytic readout in a general aging population.

title: Epicutis TCP Serum Shows 77% Skin Appearance Improvement in 8-Week Double-Blind Trial — Cellular Longevity Application
description: Epicutis published an 8-week double-blind placebo-controlled clinical study (n=48) showing 77% improvement in overall skin appearance with its TCP-powered Hydrobiome Serum versus 56% for a comparator and no measurable change for vehicle control, with zero reported irritation. TCP is a patented isoprenyl cysteine compound targeting membrane lipid signaling relevant to cellular aging and senescence biology.

title: Rockefeller University Organism-Wide Aging Atlas: 7 Million Cells Across 21 Organs Reveal Synchronized Early-Onset Decline
description: Published in Science (DOI: 10.1126/science.adw6273), Rockefeller University researchers mapped nearly 7 million individual cells across 21 mammalian organs at three ages, finding aging begins before five months and unfolds in a coordinated body-wide pattern. About 25% of cell types change in abundance over time, ~40% of changes are sex-specific, and 1,000 cross-tissue genomic hotspots linked to immune function and inflammation were identified as potential multi-organ anti-aging therapy targets. Full atlas available at epiage.net.

title: Immature Hippocampal Neurons Linked to Alzheimer's Cognitive Resilience — 30% of AD Brains Never Develop Dementia
description: A Netherlands Brain Bank study confirmed that immature neuronal populations persist in the adult human hippocampus regardless of Alzheimer's pathology, but that in cognitively resilient individuals these cells activate survival programs, show lower inflammation and apoptosis signals, and maintain transcriptional 'juvenile' functions. The findings support adult neurogenesis as a contributor to cognitive reserve and identify these cells as potential regenerative therapy targets for Alzheimer's.

title: Genflow SIRT6 Centenarian Gene Therapy Collaboration Officially Announced — LNP Delivery Fully Funded by Acuitas
description: Genflow Biosciences announced a strategic collaboration with Acuitas Therapeutics to formulate Genflow's SIRT6 centenarian-variant mRNA using Acuitas's clinically validated lipid nanoparticle system, in a fully-funded arrangement with no equity dilution. Genflow's GF-1002 program includes an ongoing proof-of-concept dog aging trial started March 2025 and a planned MASH clinical study.

title: UT Health San Antonio Launches Three-Drug Aging Trial: Rapamycin, Semaglutide, and Dapagliflozin in 700+ Adults
description: UT Health San Antonio is launching one of the largest aging intervention trials to date, testing three approved drugs — rapamycin (mTOR), semaglutide (GLP-1), and dapagliflozin (SGLT2) — in approximately 700+ older South Texans in a multi-phase NIA-funded study designed to establish evidence-based dosing and multi-pathway biological effects on aging. The multi-drug approach reflects a shift in longevity science from single-molecule to combination pathway intervention.
